rulings.Case Title: ANDREW CHEKENE ENTERPRISES INC. VS. IRVING ZARETSKY ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR WITHDRAWAL OF ATTORNEY OF RECORD
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for May 16, 2025 line 1. DEFENDANT IRVING ZARETSKY MOTION FOR WITHDRAWAL OF ATTORNEY OF RECORD is GRANTED. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified, and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: MARY ANN T. GO VS. DAVID P. CARAVANTES ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Amended Notice Of Motion For An Order Confirming Referee'S Report On Offsets
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for May 16, 2025 line 3. DEFENDANT DAVID CARAVANTES Amended Notice Of Motion For An Order Confirming Referee'S Report On Offsets is CONTINUED to May 23, 2025 for supplemental briefing. The Court has reviewed the parties' submissions made in support of and in opposition to, the motion. It also reviewed the Referee's Report and Recommendation, Plaintiff's Objection filed March 28, 2025, and the Referee's April 29, 2025 Declaration filed in response to the Objection. The Court finds the Referee's findings and recommendations are supported by law and/or the evidence submitted by the parties, with the exception of 2 issues. First, the Court does not agree with the amount awarded to Plaintiff in attorney's fees. The invoices (Appendix pages 38-62) appear to show more than $49,865.20 incurred in fees. Second, it appears that there is at least some evidence that Plaintiff made additional HELOC payments in the amount of $139.19 on March 12, 30, and June 12, 2022. (Appendix 816; Objection, 3.) It's unclear whether the Referee considered this payment. The Referee shall file a declaration no later than May 19, 2025 addressing both issues. Plaintiff may file a declaration no later than May 20 and no longer than 4 pages, in response to the Referee's findings on these issues. Defendant's request for order allowing both parties to credit bid is denied as premature. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified, and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: THOMAS PAINE SQUARE LLC VS. ANONA LEE ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT/DEFAULT JUDGMENT/LEAVE TO DEFEND
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for May 16, 2025 line 6. DEFENDANT ANONA LEE MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT/DEFAULT JUDGMENT/LEAVE TO DEFEND is GRANTED, based upon the mandatory provision for relief from default resulting from attorney fault. (CCP 473(b).) Plaintiff's late-filed opposition which was not served on defendant's counsel is stricken. The default of defendant Anona Lee is set aside. The default judgment entered on April 1, 2025 is vacated. The writ issued on April 11, 2025 is recalled. Defendant Anona Lee to file and serve the proposed response within 5 days. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849).
